mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for equity valuations and lower corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.96).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 60% of occurrences, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression over 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing consumer price inflation during early expansion phases expands corporate profit margins, triggering algorithmic momentum strategies that push S&P 500 returns higher within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered periods, or predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 280 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and forcing deleveraging that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of threshold crossings, or signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 285.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicate extreme labor market tightness, forcing firms to absorb wage inflation that erodes earnings and produces negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of joint signal occurrences, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by > 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of periods following positive GDP prints, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.01 over 36-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100 baseline index signals manufacturing contraction and weakening supply chain activity, prompting risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or signal accuracy drops below 50% when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark.
